fhir_implementation_guides.name: US Core
fhir_implementation_guides.description: Foundational US Realm IG defining minimum FHIR profile constraints implementing USCDI; required for ONC (g)(10) certification.
fhir_implementation_guides.design_pattern: National-realm base profiles + RESTful interactions

fhir_implementation_guides.name: SMART App Launch Framework
fhir_implementation_guides.description: OAuth 2.0-based launch and authorization framework for third-party apps connecting to EHR/FHIR systems; defines scopes, discovery, and Backend Services.
fhir_implementation_guides.design_pattern: OAuth 2.0 authorization + scoped access + launch context

fhir_implementation_guides.name: Bulk Data Access (Flat FHIR)
fhir_implementation_guides.description: Asynchronous, population-scale FHIR export operation supporting Group, Patient, and System-level bulk export with NDJSON output.
fhir_implementation_guides.design_pattern: Async operation + status polling + bulk file manifest

fhir_implementation_guides.name: Da Vinci Payer Data Exchange (PDex)
fhir_implementation_guides.description: Defines how health plans exchange clinical and claims-derived data with members and providers using FHIR and C-CDA on FHIR.
fhir_implementation_guides.design_pattern: Payer-to-provider/member data exchange; $everything-style operations

fhir_implementation_guides.name: Da Vinci Prior Authorization Support (PAS)
fhir_implementation_guides.description: Maps FHIR resources to X12 278 transactions to enable prior authorization requests and responses directly from EHRs to payers.
fhir_implementation_guides.design_pattern: FHIR-to-X12 mapping; Claim/$submit operation; Subscription-based responses

fhir_implementation_guides.name: Da Vinci Coverage Requirements Discovery (CRD)
fhir_implementation_guides.description: Uses CDS Hooks to surface payer coverage and documentation requirements at the point of order entry in the clinical workflow.
fhir_implementation_guides.design_pattern: CDS Hooks cards + coverage decision support

fhir_implementation_guides.name: Da Vinci Documentation Templates and Rules (DTR)
fhir_implementation_guides.description: Allows providers to download SMART questionnaires and CQL rules to gather documentation required by payers; uses Questionnaire/QuestionnaireResponse.
fhir_implementation_guides.design_pattern: SMART on FHIR app + Questionnaire/QuestionnaireResponse + CQL rules

fhir_implementation_guides.name: Da Vinci Plan Net
fhir_implementation_guides.description: FHIR API for publishing payer provider/pharmacy network directories using InsurancePlan, Organization, PractitionerRole, and Location profiles.
fhir_implementation_guides.design_pattern: Provider directory REST API + nested include search

fhir_implementation_guides.name: Da Vinci US Drug Formulary
fhir_implementation_guides.description: FHIR API to a health plan's drug formulary using InsurancePlan and MedicationKnowledge profiles, enabling patients to compare drug costs across plans.
fhir_implementation_guides.design_pattern: Formulary API + InsurancePlan/MedicationKnowledge profiles + Bulk export

fhir_implementation_guides.name: Da Vinci Clinical Data Exchange (CDex)
fhir_implementation_guides.description: Defines Direct Query, Task-based, and Attachments patterns for exchanging clinical data between providers and payers (or other providers).
fhir_implementation_guides.design_pattern: Task-based exchange + CommunicationRequest + Document attachments

fhir_implementation_guides.name: International Patient Summary (IPS)
fhir_implementation_guides.description: Minimal, specialty-agnostic patient summary document for unplanned cross-border care, using internationally reusable value sets.
fhir_implementation_guides.design_pattern: FHIR Document (Composition) + international value sets

fhir_implementation_guides.name: International Patient Access (IPA)
fhir_implementation_guides.description: Minimal base set of FHIR profiles and RESTful interactions enabling patient access to their data worldwide, paired with SMART on FHIR for authorization.
fhir_implementation_guides.design_pattern: RESTful access + SMART App Launch + minimal profiles

fhir_implementation_guides.name: mCODE (Minimal Common Oncology Data Elements)
fhir_implementation_guides.description: Approximately 40 FHIR profiles providing a core set of structured oncology data elements, organized into six thematic groups.
fhir_implementation_guides.design_pattern: Domain-specific profile set + MustSupport conformance + multiple profile groups

fhir_implementation_guides.name: Structured Data Capture (SDC)
fhir_implementation_guides.description: Infrastructure for Questionnaire/QuestionnaireResponse with pre-population, advanced rendering, calculations, adaptive forms, and extraction capabilities.
fhir_implementation_guides.design_pattern: Questionnaire-based forms + expression-based behavior + StructureMap extraction

fhir_implementation_guides.name: Quality Improvement Core (QI-Core)
fhir_implementation_guides.description: Set of FHIR profiles and extensions for clinical quality measurement (eCQM) and decision support, harmonizing QDM and vMR data requirements.
fhir_implementation_guides.design_pattern: Quality-measurement profiles + QDM mapping + CQL-ready

fhir_implementation_guides.name: Genomics Reporting Implementation Guide
fhir_implementation_guides.description: FHIR representations of variants, genotypes, pharmacogenomics, somatic and histocompatibility (HLA) reporting using Observation and DiagnosticReport.
fhir_implementation_guides.design_pattern: Domain-specific Observation/DiagnosticReport profiles + hasMember/derivedFrom graphs

fhir_implementation_guides.name: Electronic Case Reporting (eCR)
fhir_implementation_guides.description: Automated public health case reporting from EHRs to health departments and reportability response reports back, using trigger codes and ClinicalDocument Composition.
fhir_implementation_guides.design_pattern: Public health reporting + trigger code subscriptions + Composition/Document

fhir_implementation_guides.name: Subscriptions R5 Backport
fhir_implementation_guides.description: Backports R5 topic-based Subscription framework (SubscriptionTopic, rest-hook, Bundle notifications) to R4 and R4B servers.
fhir_implementation_guides.design_pattern: Backporting newer FHIR features via extensions + SubscriptionTopic + topic-based subscription

fhir_implementation_guides.name: AU Core
fhir_implementation_guides.description: Australian national FHIR conformance baseline; profiles and RESTful API setting minimum expectations for patient data access in Australia.
fhir_implementation_guides.design_pattern: National-realm base profiles (AU analog to US Core) + AU Base dependencies

fhir_implementation_guides.name: CDS Hooks
fhir_implementation_guides.description: Hook-based pattern for invoking decision support from within a clinician's workflow via synchronous REST calls returning cards; companion to FHIR but not a pure FHIR resource IG.
fhir_implementation_guides.design_pattern: Hook-based REST + cards + prefetch templates + SMART launch
